EmailOtpSenderServiceSettings.Subject: Your DC SUN Bucks Login Code

BlockedAddresses: 2100 Martin Luther King Jr Avenue SE

_socure_comment: ApiKey: obtain from Socure RiskOS dashboard > Developer Workbench > API Keys (sandbox and production are different).
WebhookSecret: the Bearer token Socure sends in webhook Authorization headers. Set in Socure dashboard under Webhook settings.

Webhook URL allowlist: Socure sandbox only delivers webhooks to URLs that have been pre-registered in the dashboard. For local development, expose the API via ngrok (or equivalent) and register the public URL (e.g. https://<hash>.ngrok.io/api/socure/webhook) in the Socure dashboard under Webhook settings. Without this step the docv status will never transition past 'pending' — the SDK and backend are healthy but terminal verdicts never arrive.
UseStub: set to false to use the real Socure API. When true, uses a local stub that always returns DocumentVerificationRequired.
DocvTransactionTokenTtlMinutes: after this many minutes from issuance, StartChallenge requests a new DocV token via Socure using the stored id-proofing inputs on the challenge (default 20).

DiSessionToken: fallback for when the frontend DI SDK is not integrated. Any value works in sandbox (e.g., 'sandbox-placeholder'). In production, the frontend sends a real token and this fallback is not used.
SandboxPhoneOverride: when set, overrides the user's phone number in Socure requests so DocV SMS links go to your real phone. Format: +1XXXXXXXXXX. Leave null or remove in production.
